passedAssertions.reason: Same-session preflight reached userinfo 200 and Sheets metadata 200, the signed-in drawer exposed write-capable CRM controls, and a real Sheets write to Pipeline!O4 returned 200.
After the configured OAuth client ID changed, the cached oauth session marker was cleared and the app returned to the Continue/sign-in gate instead of silently reusing the old session.
Forcing the next Sheets write to return 401 cleared auth state, showed a session-expired sign-in-again toast, hid account chrome, and returned the app to the write-disabled gate.

From zero saved app state in an authenticated browser context, starter-sheet creation stored the new sheet ID, onboarding intercepted before deferred discovery resumed, and the discovery wizard reopened exactly once after onboarding completion.

failedAssertions.reason: With no valid sheet ID and no usable OAuth bootstrap path, Save & reload still exited Settings and reloaded to the top-level access surface instead of keeping the Sheet tab open with remediation.
Both discovery-focused entry points remained no-ops: the dashboard button and Settings → Discovery button left the URL unchanged and never opened a visible discovery setup surface.
Reloading with the same OAuth client ID dropped back to the Continue/sign-in gate, removed account chrome, and cleared command_center_oauth_session instead of silently restoring write access.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated authenticated rerun guidance to use the saved browser state path plus manual same-session userinfo 200 and Sheets 200 preflight, and documented that bridge-based write access may still need a gcloud-backed userinfo probe on this machine.

systemicBlockers.rootCause: Same-client OAuth session restore still fails closed after reload. The authenticated state established in-session does not survive a page reload for the unchanged client ID.
systemicBlockers.suggestedResolution: Investigate why command_center_oauth_session is cleared on same-client reload and why the app falls back to the Continue/sign-in gate instead of silently restoring write access.

systemicBlockers.rootCause: The invalid-sheet save path still treats a broken configuration as a full-page reload target rather than an in-Settings validation error.
systemicBlockers.suggestedResolution: Keep Settings open on the Sheet tab when both sheet ID and OAuth bootstrap are invalid, and surface explicit remediation before any reload.

systemicBlockers.rootCause: Discovery-focused setup entry points still do not dispatch a visible setup surface from either dashboard or Settings.
systemicBlockers.suggestedResolution: Wire both discovery entry points to the actual discovery setup/wizard surface and verify they are not hidden behind a no-op state transition.
